title: 10 Best Active Constituents of Ulmus rubra (Slippery Elm)

constituents.constituent_desc: The gallic acid contained in Ulmus Rubra is a type of polyphenolic compound that has potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties. Its main medicinal properties include its ability to neutralize free radicals, reduce inflammation, and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Gallic acid has been shown to help relieve various health conditions such as gastrointestinal disorders, skin irritations, and respiratory ailments. This constituent is found mainly in the bark and leaves of the Ulmus rubra plant. However, when used improperly, gallic acid can cause adverse effects such as nausea, stomach upset, and skin irritation. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using slippery elm or its constituents for medicinal purposes.

The ellagic acid contained in Ulmus Rubra is a type of polyphenolic compound that has been identified as having several medicinal properties. Its main medicinal properties include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer effects, making it a valuable constituent in various pharmaceutical and nutritional applications. Ulmus rubra ellagic acid has been shown to help relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, including inflammatory bowel disease, ulcers, and constipation. It is found mainly in the bark and inner bark of the slippery elm tree, and can be extracted through various methods such as decoction, infusion, or tincture. Improper use of Ulmus rubra ellagic acid can lead to several side effects, including nausea, stomach discomfort, and allergic reactions. Therefore, it is important to follow recommended dosages and consult with a healthcare professional before using slippery elm or its constituents for medicinal purposes.

The quercetin contained in Ulmus Rubra is a type of flavonoid, a group of plant pigments with antioxidant properties. It is known for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties. Quercetin helps relieve symptoms of various health conditions such as allergies, inflammation, and heart disease. It is found in the bark, leaves, and inner bark of the slippery elm tree. Improper use of quercetin can lead to side effects such as headaches, tingling sensations, and upset stomach.

The kaempferol contained in Ulmus Rubra is a type of flavonoid, a group of plant compounds with diverse beneficial properties. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer effects, making it a valuable constituent in various herbal remedies. Kaempferol has been found to help relieve several health conditions, such as inflammatory diseases, cardiovascular disorders, and certain types of cancer, by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the proliferation of cancer cells. This active compound is primarily found in the inner bark of the Ulmus rubra tree, which is used to make slippery elm supplements, teas, and lozenges. Improper use of kaempferol may lead to several side effects, including digestive issues, headaches, and potential interactions with certain medications, such as anticoagulants and antiplatelet drugs.

The myricetin contained in Ulmus Rubra is a type of flavonoid, a class of compounds with antioxidant properties. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer effects. Myricetin has been shown to help relieve various health conditions, such as gastrointestinal disorders, inflammatory diseases, and cancer. This constituent is found mainly in the bark of the Ulmus rubra plant. Some possible side effects of myricetin, if used improperly, include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions.

The isoquercitrin contained in Ulmus Rubra is a flavonoid known for its antioxidant properties. It has anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and anticancer properties, making it a valuable constituent in this plant. Isoquercitrin has been found to help relieve symptoms of various health conditions, including respiratory ailments, digestive issues, and skin irritations. This compound is found mainly in the inner bark of the slippery elm tree, which is harvested and processed into various forms such as powder, capsules, and teas. Improper use of isoquercitrin may cause side effects such as stomach discomfort, nausea, and allergic reactions, so it is important to follow recommended dosages and consult with a healthcare provider before use.

The astragalin contained in Ulmus Rubra is a flavonoid that boasts several medicinal properties. It has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties, making it a valuable constituent in many natural remedies. Astragalin is known to help relieve conditions such as inflammation, oxidative stress, and certain types of cancer. It can be found in various parts of the Ulmus rubra plant, including the bark, leaves, and inner bark. Improper use of astragalin can lead to several side effects, including digestive issues, skin irritation, and in some cases, allergic reactions.

The rutin contained in Ulmus rubra is a type of flavonoid, a group of plant compounds with antioxidant properties. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. Rutin has been shown to help relieve conditions such as inflammation, high blood pressure, and viral infections. This constituent is found mainly in the bark of the Ulmus rubra tree. Some possible side effects of rutin, if used improperly, include stomach upset, skin irritation, and headache.

The hyperoside contained in Ulmus Rubra is a type of flavonoid, a class of active compounds with various health benefits. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting effects. This constituent can help relieve various health conditions, such as digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations. Hyperoside is found mainly in the inner bark of the Ulmus rubra tree, which is used to make various herbal remedies. However, if used improperly or in large amounts, hyperoside can cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interference with certain medications.

The ulmoside a contained in Ulmus Rubra is a triterpene compound with potential anti-inflammatory, demulcent, and emollient properties. Its main medicinal properties include soothing and protecting mucous membranes, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from coughs and sore throats. Ulmoside A has been found to help relieve symptoms of various health conditions, such as digestive disorders, respiratory ailments, and skin irritations. This constituent is primarily found in the inner bark of the Ulmus rubra tree, which is used to make herbal remedies and supplements. Improper use of ulmoside a may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive discomfort, and potential interactions with other medications.

intro: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, contains several active constituents that have medicinal properties. The bark of the tree is the primary source of these constituents, which include mucilage, tannins, starch, calcium oxalate, and a variety of nutrients. The mucilage is a complex mixture of polysaccharides that can soothe and protect mucous membranes, making it useful for treating digestive issues such as heartburn, acid reflux, and gastritis. Tannins, on the other hand, have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The antioxidant properties of slippery elm may also provide additional health benefits, such as boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of chronic diseases. However, it is important to note that more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of Ulmus rubra and its active constituents.
